task.title: Configure a publishing workflow
task.shortdesc: Create a branch, edit a topic, validate the draft, and publish changes when the content is ready.

context.p: Use this task when testing process-oriented template regions. The steps provide clear ordered content for body and card layouts.

step.cmd: Select the project branch from the Git panel.

info.p: The branch controls which repository version the Explorer displays.

step.cmd: Open the topic that needs an update.

info.p: The editor loads the current draft from Postgres or fetches the file from the provider if needed.
Validation uses DITA-OT and includes referenced topics and images in the temporary validation workspace.

step.cmd: Commit the selected files and publish to origin.

info.p: If GitHub changed since the draft was created, the system blocks publishing and opens the conflict resolver.

result.p: The branch contains a validated commit that can be reviewed or published to the configured provider.
